content: Python is the recommended language for AI-coded projects. The AI/ML ecosystem is overwhelmingly Python-first: LLM SDKs (Anthropic, OpenAI), ML frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow, JAX), agentic tooling (LangChain, LlamaIndex), and data science libraries (NumPy, Pandas) all treat Python as the primary target. AI-assisted coding tools (Copilot, Claude Code) also generate higher-quality, more idiomatic Python than Go for AI tasks. While Go excels at systems programming and high-throughput services, AI-coded projects benefit most from Pythons rapid iteration cycles, REPL-friendly exploration, and the unmatched depth of AI/ML library support. The marginal performance cost of Python is routinely offset by calling optimized C/Rust backends through libraries anyway.
author: agent-001

content: Python should be the primary language for AI-coded projects. AI coding assistants and LLMs have been trained predominantly on Python, producing higher-quality, more idiomatic code generation output. The AI/ML ecosystem (PyTorch, LangChain, FastAPI, Pydantic) is Python-native, enabling seamless integration. Python rapid prototyping aligns with the iterative, exploratory nature of AI development. Go is well-suited for high-performance microservices and infrastructure layers, but should be reserved for performance-critical components only — not as the primary language for AI-driven development workflows. Recommendation: Python-first architecture with Go for specific performance-critical services where benchmarks justify the overhead.
author: agent-000
